title: Lock views
excerpt:
Hi all 
In several of our boards, we have set up custom views so that only the columns we want our users to interact with are visible. If they flip to the ‘main table’ view, it includes a bunch of columns that ordinary u…

posts.author: carolinem (Caroline McKinnon)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi all

In several of our boards, we have set up custom views so that only the columns we want our users to interact with are visible. If they flip to the ‘main table’ view, it includes a bunch of columns that ordinary users of that board will not understand or use (in some cases they contain info necessary to make certain automations work, for example).

We desperately need a way to hide the ‘main table’ view. I am aware that we can set a different view as the default, but there is no way to drag and drop the main table to the end of the list of available views, or better yet, just hide it altogether from anyone who is not an admin or board owner, which is what we’d prefer.

This would be a very useful feature to add.

posts.author: djbeame (Bernard Wilson)
posts.cookedHtml:
Yes - agree with this.

For advanced boards, the main table view is more like an admin view. It wouldn’t be great if this main table view could have an option to hide for all users except for the admin.

posts.author: Kitty (Katelyn Holt)
posts.cookedHtml: This would be Extremely useful for us. We use complex boards and filter what users see via multiple different views. The main table is purely for admin purposes and it would be great if we had the ability to hide it, as right now it’s accessible to all, and confusing to users who accidently open it due to all the extra columns used to power automations and board to board links.

posts.author: John.Corbly7 (John Corbly)
posts.cookedHtml: I completely agree with this. The Main Table often times only offers more opportunity for confusion amongst the majority of our users.

posts.author: SRM_Travis (Travis Salyer)
posts.cookedHtml: Agreed - this would be extremely useful to set it up for admin purposes only, but hidden from general viewing. Some people do not realize that the view we want them to see they actually have to click the tab to change views, and it generates more confusion/questions than it helps sometimes.

posts.author: McKolm (Michael Kolmanovich)
posts.cookedHtml: As a new user of Monday, I see similar request pop up during few last years. What’s happening, @Becca-monday.com ? You’ve closed some of those requests. Any updates on implementation?

posts.author: TEAMVAERK (Morten)
posts.cookedHtml: Very much agree on this. For us its going to be an admin-view where as other views will be used day to day.

posts.author: j.zirklewski (Jörg)
posts.cookedHtml:
Didn’t miss this feature - until today.
Setting up a large ticket board with much information per ticket.
We have a team that priorises the tickets and adds a responsible person.
And then we have the development team with the focus of only assigned tickets and details the customer submitted.
And the CEO wants to see all overdue tickets and does not want to see all the details.

All of them should at least have an own default view or only one customized view

posts.author: Soeren (Soeren)
posts.cookedHtml: This would be a great feature. Especially since we have guests who should not see every table…

posts.author: biograf (Chris)
posts.cookedHtml: Would be a great feature for me too!

posts.author: Gorntanagrit.t (Gorntanagrit)
posts.cookedHtml: an upgrade on this feature will be useful to many member. If possible, adding the view restriction to selected member which categorize as different department or (team) at the view tab. Highly appreciated if Monday team planned to launch this feature soon.

posts.author: awright (Amanda)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes, I agree with this. I would love to hide the Main Table view from all members.

posts.author: thidaworkman (Thida Workman)
posts.cookedHtml: Agree, hiding the Main Table would be ideal.

posts.author: FdL (Franck de Luca)
posts.cookedHtml:
Fully agree. It would be also useful that each user could have a different default view.
Some of our boards are used y different types of users: technicians on the road, scheduler in the office, managers, … They all need different views with different filters, but there is only one default view, so one of the groups has the right default view and all other groups of users must annoyingly change view each time they go on a board. This does not help having a nice customer experience.
Thanks to improve this as soon as you can …

posts.author: iamvenumous (Chris Hollingsworth)
posts.cookedHtml: 100% agree on hiding the Main Table or at least re-ordering the views so it can be placed last. This would be one of the best implementations that we could imagine.

posts.author: pinar (Pinar Crombie)
posts.cookedHtml: 100% agree as well. If at least we can reorder the Views and put Main table at the end, this would be helpful. Currently there is no way to reorder the Views. Not sure if there is a separate request for that.

posts.author: RyanBrown (Ryan Brown)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes please. Would really love this feature.

posts.author: Jan-Hendrik (Jan-Hendrik)
posts.cookedHtml:
Agreed.

Views (especially main table) should be:

  1. hideable
  2. sortable (e.g. per drag & drop)

posts.author: KennethGee (Kenneth Lehn Gee)
posts.cookedHtml: Agree, I really need this as well.

posts.author: Kmk (kmk)
posts.cookedHtml: So logical feature for what monday.com aim to do…

posts.author: lindsaydreece (Lindsay Reece)
posts.cookedHtml: Did anybody get a response or work around to this yet?

posts.author: croman653 (CR)
posts.cookedHtml: I agree. Another huge missing feature that is a big source of frustration. There are times that not everyone should be able to see every view, and I should be able to set that as the board owner.

posts.author: carolinem (Caroline McKinnon)
posts.cookedHtml: Unfortunately, no, not yet.

posts.author: ghostyslv (Svetoslav Slavchev)
posts.cookedHtml: I hope this comes to live soon. It will be fantastic to have it! It will solve many problems!

posts.author: mrjberlin (Jason Berlin)
posts.cookedHtml:
I created a solution for those using Chrome, though Firefox browser could do the same.

Hoping the team will implement something like this in an official manner.

They could also make it dynamic, based on user-role (Workspace Owner, Admin, People, Users)!

FYI: The same solution would work towards hiding filters from users but require additional and different code to target that class.

Hide the Main Table View Solution via JS/CSS & Perhaps Future App? monday Apps & Developers
For those that are familiar with JS/CSS, there are ways to hide specific elements on a website. On Chrome, specifically, I’ve had my team use the following extension: I then added the following CSS .monday-board-subsets-tabs .monday-board-subset-item-v2:nth-child(2) { display: none; }``` This makes it so that the **second HTML Div / view** gets hidden. I did this because I have an overview set as default... Now this code clearly hides the main table view for everyone. Not sure if an app …

posts.author: mcouger (Mechele Couger)
posts.cookedHtml:
YES! We have many columns on our main boards that are there purely for admin purposes. For example:
* date columns that are there simply to feed into a formula that displays the preferred format
* columns needed for automations but not for display
* columns needed for dashboards but not for display
Although we have other views set as the default, the title “Main Table” leads our users to believe this is the main/more complete view they should look at which leads to unnecessary confusion. We desperately need to be able to hide the main board!

posts.author: awright (Amanda)
posts.cookedHtml: I really would like to see this feature implemented.

posts.author: Rihards (Rihards Donis)
posts.cookedHtml: Is there any updates on this? Who is controling this and when can something like this be implemented?

posts.author: awright (Amanda)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi, Jason. I added the extension, but where do I put the CSS script you shared?

posts.author: awright (Amanda)
posts.cookedHtml: Jason, I figured out to do what you recommened and it worked perfectly. Thank you for sharing this workaround!!

posts.author: scottmorley (Scott Morley)
posts.cookedHtml: This would be a very useful feature, along with the ability to re-order the tabs for the various board views as desired.

posts.author: dwadsworth (Dustin Wadsworth)
posts.cookedHtml:
Serious flaw here. Please Monday.com resolve this. If I click the notification for a new item it opens one view, if I go to the board first then open the item I see something different.

I see different fields depending on which device I am on, and how I navigate to the overview area.

This is a critical user experience flaw that has paused implementation of Monday.com for my business.

posts.author: bgreen (Betsy Green)
posts.cookedHtml: Add me to the growing list of people who would appreciate this feature!

posts.author: jennistrong34 (Jennifer Gallant)
posts.cookedHtml: Is there an update on this? We really need this feature.

posts.author: jailynnhoops (Jai-Lynn Hoops)
posts.cookedHtml: This would be incredibly helpful! Even as simple as a hide main table from members vs. Board Owners would be great!

posts.author: deena (deenateichman)
posts.cookedHtml: Count me in here!! Would be very helpful. Also @monday while you’re at this can you work on the filtering “save to this view” and not allow any user to just switch up filters that are set. It should be an admin permission only to “save to this view” for everyone.

posts.author: ReturnOfTheMac (M-A C)
posts.cookedHtml: Hello everyone who participated in this thread or are watchers, don’t forget tpo click the “vote” button at the top of the thread to show this is a priority for you! I just did, didn’t know Monday was cleaning up their community board so added my vote to this one as well.

posts.author: lhoughton (Laura)
posts.cookedHtml: this is pretty crazy. 1) no ability to move or hide a master column that has a ton of columns that aren’t useful to anyone except the admins who built the board. Inability to reorder views means we cant even drag it to the end - crazy crazy crazy

posts.author: Rangler (Simon Engelmann)
posts.cookedHtml: please implement this function, that would be a milestone.

posts.author: Kelsey_Sylvester78 (Kelsey Sylvester)
posts.cookedHtml: My team has discussed this same feature! It would be so helpful!

posts.author: rajwilkhu (Raj Wilkhu)
posts.cookedHtml: We really need this feature. Ideally would like to hide the list of members to Monday.com to all users.

posts.author: cbacon (Caleb Bacon)
posts.cookedHtml: I think it would be a good idea to not hide columns from users on the main board but to only allow them to see a certain saved view based on their user type. Ex: Guests can only see the guest/simplified view. Members get access to a more detailed view…

posts.author: cbacon (Caleb Bacon)
posts.cookedHtml: Or at least a default view.

posts.author: jk193 (Jake K)
posts.cookedHtml: +1 to this for both ability to set the default view on a board/project, and also restricting guest users to only certain views. Would be very helpful to allow clients a simplified dashboard view or kanban view with certain filters etc, and not let them change the view or filter settings. HUGE problem working with clients - Monday is too complex for them and they get confused quickly.

posts.author: jk193 (Jake K)
posts.cookedHtml:
This is somewhat related to Feature Request: Private Notes / Hide from Guests - #7 by DF2019
Though I think in general it would be nice to have more generalizable permission settings to hide an entire item, group, or columns from guest users, rather than just updates/info boxes on a pulse as that thread indicates.

posts.author: cmulligan07 (Christi Mulligan)
posts.cookedHtml:
@monday-team @monday-moderators @CXS — has there been any movement on this? Is this something that is part of the new board design/experience. I didn’t see it called out in literature but am curious where this sits. I understand hidden columns and how you can do this with a Dashboard vs. a dashboard view BUT just wondering if there is a way to make this work.

I am the Director of Learning and Development for Big10 University in the School of Social Work with my state gov DCFS as my client.

Internally, I have a full multi-phase project work plan that I have my leadership team on so they see the big picture - my designers, different reviewers (i.e. copy edit, ADA, etc) who I only want to view one phase of the plan at a time to stay focused during sprints but also want them to see high level vision on the dashboard. Additionally, I will eventually want client-facing dashboards that offer a different high level look at the work being done.

I know i can do a lot with automations, mirroring, etc. but a simpler way would be to be able to provide my wider team access to a cards/Kanban, etc. view with only that phase featured, etc. Hopefully this makes since and adds color to what’s already been here since July 2020

I understand board permissions overall as well - but this is beyond hidden columns or what’s available via board permissions.

posts.author: cmulligan07 (Christi Mulligan)
posts.cookedHtml: Also different than internal communications here

posts.author: johngcolejr (John Cole, Jr)
posts.cookedHtml: Do we have movement on this? Is Monday! working on implementing it? We have set views for daily meetings, and users can modify them causing significant visibility issues. most of the changes are a result of someone not knowing what they are doing. In my mind, its one more level of security between modify and view only your content.

posts.author: cbacon (Caleb Bacon)
posts.cookedHtml: I think it would be a good idea to not hide columns from users on the main board but to only allow them to see a certain saved view based on their user type. Ex: Guests can only see the guest/simplified view. Members get access to a more detailed view…

posts.author: clayanderson (Clay Anderson)
posts.cookedHtml:
I would love to either (a) be able to modify the “Main Table” view for all users, or (b) set a default view for all members, so that they don’t see the Main Table when they visit a board.

We have a lot of folks in our organization who need to view our boards, but we have a lot of additional IT-specific fields in our boards that non-IT folks don’t need to see. Would be great to simplify the view that most folks see.

posts.author: KieranBiscoe (Kieran)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi, Is there a way to have a default view be set at a user level rather than a board level? I have a board which is used by two members of our team and they both need to see different columns but on the same item, I can set a board default view but that only helps one person.

posts.author: smedina (Steven Medina)
posts.cookedHtml: Please allow us to create and edit views that can only be seen by the user that created it. As of right now, all the views I create and edit are seen by our whole company. This is not good if I want to do complex filtering of a large amount of pulses.

posts.author: alejencia (Alex Becerra)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi smedina,

I’ve created and saved filters for users and when the user goes to that board it defaults to their view. Wondering if you are referring to something else?

Hope to help.

posts.author: smedina (Steven Medina)
posts.cookedHtml:
Thank you for responding!

The issue I can’t seem to solve is if I create View, everyone in my company can see it. This means that if 30 employees create a view, I see 29 views that I do not care about, and one view that I do care about.

Can I create a new view than no one else can see?

posts.author: alejencia (Alex Becerra)
posts.cookedHtml:
smedina,

I totally get what you are referring to. I don’t know of any ways to do that. Monday.com would be best to address your concern.

posts.author: Julia-monday.com (Julia Fagelman)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @smedina

Thank you for the feedback
Creating personal views is on our roadmap so stay tuned for updates on that

posts.author: smedina (Steven Medina)
posts.cookedHtml:
Thank you!

Love the overalls and balloon combo in the profile pic. It’s kinda like “yea i’m fun but I know what I’m talkin’ about”.

posts.author: Julia-monday.com (Julia Fagelman)
posts.cookedHtml: Haha, thank you Steven - glad that’s the impression I’m giving

posts.author: j.cevallos (Javier Cevallos)
posts.cookedHtml: Hey @Julia-monday.com , is there anything on the roadmap for controlling permissions for teams/users and controlling access to views/dasboards in the v2 api . I am just getting started with evaluating the automation we can put behind your api and am finding little information other than board subscriptions in the v1 api . Would love to talk about this .

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi all,

We sincerely apologise for the delay in responding to this thread! We are working on improving our responsiveness to the community, particularly our feature request process so appreciate your patience in advance!

On that note, as we pull together all the votes associated with this topic, we would love if you can redirect the votes on this thread, to the primary thread (most voted) here: Set default views for all users and hide main table view - as we plan to close this smaller thread in an attempt to consolidate all votes, and better prioritise this feature request based upon the total amount of votes

Thanks!

posts.author: uubee (William Booker)
posts.cookedHtml:
working on building a handful of saved views for our super board to help narrow down a couple specific workflows.

pro: views are saved across our organization so we can set up a couple workflows that should work for everyone.
con: it is way too easy for someone to add/remove a filter and overwrite the saved view for everyone else.

is there a way to lock down a saved view for the team on a specific board so they can add filters for themselves (ie search for themselves to see what is assigned to them) but won’t wreck the saved view for everyone else?

posts.author: JohnW (John Waller)
posts.cookedHtml:
uubee:

con: it is way too easy for someone to add/remove a filter and overwrite the saved view for everyone else.

Agreed. I do exactly that myself if I’m not fully concentrating on what I’m doing.

posts.author: horton (Chris)
posts.cookedHtml:
+1. We want both group-level views (everyone’s projects) and person-specific views (my projects filtered from the team board) on our projects board. Only admins should be able to change the group-level views. For person-specific views, ideally only admins and specific users can see/change them.

An alternative that I have used on other platforms is a variable in the view/report that represents the current user. For example, this would filter a person column for the current logged in user. That way a single view gives everyone their own list of rows.

posts.author: lrobert (Ludovic ROBERT)
posts.cookedHtml:
+1
Need a way to lock board views and prevent users from creating some.

posts.author: elena (elena)
posts.cookedHtml: And even worst prevent users deleting views. An external user can delete a view.

posts.author: jennap (Jenna)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @uubee ~ Welcome to the Community!

While we do not currently have the capability of “locking” a board view’s filters, I am going to pass your thoughts to our product team and bring this thread to their attention so future support can also be taken into account.

They take user feedback very seriously! In fact, many of our features began as user requests

I hope this helps! Remember to mark as solved if this answers your question.

Cheers,
Jenna

posts.author: mw01 (MW)
posts.cookedHtml: This is severely needed. Have had multiple team members delete our views and put us behind the 8 ball.

posts.author: Valentin (Valentin Hoyer)
posts.cookedHtml: We are another company, completely new on Monday and are looking for this feature severely. We need a way to prevent normal users from seeing all information, only admins and managers should have access to!!

posts.author: adepaul (Alex)
posts.cookedHtml: This is needed! Any plans to implement it?

posts.author: Always2Frustrated (Ryan Cooper)
posts.cookedHtml: This is such an obvious need. Most employees don’t know what the heck they’re doing. the point of views is that we can tailor what they see to be only what they need to see - nothing less/more. If they screw it up - especially the tech-illiterate ones - they screw up everyone’s workflow.

posts.author: Jan-Hendrik (Jan-Hendrik)
posts.cookedHtml: Absolutely must have feature. Dashboards that are configurable by all users are more or less useless for our company.

posts.author: cberman (Corey)
posts.cookedHtml: hey @jennap, this was 1 year ago, any update? Since rolling out monday in my company I have found that users overwrite saved views all the time, but the views are critical for the workflow of the board. We really need a way to set permissions on who can edit a view. Additionally, if people could have “personal” saved views that only they see, I think that would help as well.

posts.author: anon29275264 (anon29275264)
posts.cookedHtml: The lack of an ability for board owners to control board views to ensure a stable end-user experience is a big weakness. Currently only restricting users to editing items they are assigned to ALSO limits ability to change views. This makes very little sense. View permissions should be tied to the same permission as the ability to create automations on a board.

posts.author: Kelsey_Sylvester78 (Kelsey Sylvester)
posts.cookedHtml: I hope this feature is added soon. Some employees that use this find themselves on views that they shouldn’t be on and get confused.

posts.author: dcscott (Daniel Scott)
posts.cookedHtml: I manage boards for a non-profit with thousands of volunteers in various roles. Filtered views are essential to our operation. I spend a lot of time fixing changes to preset filtered views and even more time training volunteers on how to avoid changing the views. Locking down the ability to change shared filtered views would be a huge plus for us!

posts.author: Julietteb (Juliette )
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi community! Thanks to all those that voted and shared feedback. Great minds think alike – our development team is already working on the ability to create personal views, reorder tabs and hide views. We will keep you updated when it is ready to be released.

Requests like yours help guide us in prioritizing the features that our customers care about the most. So keep the ideas coming because your feedback impacts hundreds of thousands monday.com customers who will enjoy default views on their boards because of you.

Have another idea? Have another idea? Submit it here.

posts.author: gdufau (DUFAU)
posts.cookedHtml: We really need this feature. Hide the view by selected group or selected person

posts.author: theshiftybadger (Chase Baker)
posts.cookedHtml: this is a pretty huge feature request for us as a new customer as well. didn’t realize we couldn’t hide views from groups of users, makes it a real pain with all of our custom views in random order and views that don’t relate to each employee role.

posts.author: Kalyani (Kalyani)
posts.cookedHtml:
This is a must and one of the basic feature that should be available for all users. I see so many use cases would be achieved by this single feature update.
@monday-team do we have any update on this.

posts.author: JulieT32 (Julie Thomas)
posts.cookedHtml: Is there an update on when this will be released? It appears an essential requirement for quite a number of customers. Thanks

posts.author: kdunts (Kittty)
posts.cookedHtml: yep - can we get a timeline on this please @monday-team ?

posts.author: datea-lukas (Lukas )
posts.cookedHtml:
We need this feature aswell!

Is there a way to lock down a saved view for the team on a specific board so they can add filters for themselves (ie search for themselves to see what is assigned to them) but won’t wreck the saved view for everyone else?

posts.author: kaseybistro (Kaseyb)
posts.cookedHtml: Any updates or recent workarounds ?

posts.author: Cyrille (L)
posts.cookedHtml:
For us, it’s one of the main pet peeves that does not go in favor of Monday in our organisation. End users are confused with all the views and columns. In a board, we should be able to show only one or 2 views to a certain group of users. It gests worse, if you want to invite an outside supplier to a specific board.
The other one is when a new column is added in a board, that column is added by default to all board filters. This should be a opt-in situation, not a opt-out. (I know I can hide columns to certains users but it’s still a pain).
And why can’t we lock the tabs in a specific order for the user? At least, let us reorder them like we would with tabs in a web browser.

Our running joke is that Monday is always this close to being really great. I love Monday, but those are 2 examples of the little things that could be so much better.

posts.author: TFaust (Timo Faust)
posts.cookedHtml: Like the rest in here, hoping that we will get some update on a release time soon

posts.author: bryank (bryan k)
posts.cookedHtml: any update on this? This feature is desperately needed!

posts.author: paupau (Pau)
posts.cookedHtml: I wish monday would invest more into improving their existing features rather than just coming up with more and more features. Maybe try hiring someone from Airtable’s team

posts.author: Posty89 (Pat rafferty)
posts.cookedHtml:
Is there an update on this? Or an idea when this will be available?

I am trying to sell monday to my colleagues but this is one of the main issues that is confusing them

Please please please can we get this feature soon

posts.author: Julietteb (Juliette )
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi everyone just an update here! I spoke with our product managers and they let me know that our support for lock views will be coming out very shortly while reordering and hiding is planned and estimate to be released early next year.

Hang tights! Our team is working hard on getting these features live.

posts.author: wypca (Michelle G)
posts.cookedHtml: I agree with this and will add the ability to limit views to specific members. For example, dashboards and calendar available to view only by Group A, Filtered view available to view and modify by Group B. Master view available to Admin Group only.

posts.author: Cyrille (L)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi, for now in a board, when a new column is added, the column is added to all views automatically. Does Monday plan to add a configuration option for this? Or is it planned to change it so we have to manually add the column to existing views? When we add columns into a board, it is very tedious to remove them from all the views individually. It’s confusing users if we leave them there. As for the locking of a view that’s great and a start. Cant wait to see the full rollout of the new ‘Monday 2.0’

posts.author: arnoldprasad (Arnold P)
posts.cookedHtml: It would be good to also add permissions on views while they are at it. Thanks. Wow 2 years to implement this feature.

posts.author: Horizonsong (Karo Zieminski)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes! I need this so much!

posts.author: Kalyani (Kalyani)
posts.cookedHtml: Finally, I am glad to see this feature is on the product roadmap. Although I wish this was available sooner and we did not have to wait until the next year.

posts.author: SophieCaines (Sophie Caines)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Juliette - great to hear!

I was wondering if this would also allow us to set different default views for different users? For example, we have a board that contains all information for all teams, but would like to set the default view for each specific team, as that team’s filtered view.

posts.author: cphu (Christine Phu)
posts.cookedHtml: An ability to re-order views would be helpful as well

posts.author: ocb_app (Marcin Synowiec)
posts.cookedHtml: The feature is available now! I can’t find any release notes mentioning it though.

posts.author: Soroh (Soroh Donn)
posts.cookedHtml: Can this be added to the main table? I have users who aren’t allowed to view column info from the main table, yet I have no way of restricting access for this.

posts.author: JHconsultancy (Joseph Honig)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @Julietteb,

Will we be able to have this feature on the Main Table?

Thanks in advance.

posts.author: Julietteb (Juliette )
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Community!

Thank you for your feedback! i have an exciting update. We have developed and released the ability to lock views and are working on the private views feature which is on our long-term roadmap and estimated to be available in 2024 ( subject to change).

Thank you for being part of our development process and keep sharing your ideas with us!

posts.author: Filurisen (Therese)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi,

Fantastic news! Although I can´t find the setting in all tabs (i.e. “Main table” and a tab for Docugen settings) - are this setting only available for some tabs? The ability for non-board owners / admins to be able to edit the “Main table” (or even better, not to see it at all) is crucial for us so this is not an optimal solution unfortunately.

Kind regards, Therese

posts.author: girlinabillion (Amanda)
posts.cookedHtml: Upvote for the private views feature!

posts.author: avp (Amanda)
posts.cookedHtml: It appears that using “Lock View” prevents users from exporting the data associated with the view. Can you please separate Export from Lock View? I want to use Lock View to prevent my users from accidentally rearranging the order of widgets or from saving different filters, but its crucial that they be able to export the data.

posts.author: requity (Dylan Marma)
posts.cookedHtml:
Bianca Taylor:

t the votes on this thread, to the prima

Agreed! This would be super valuable. Do we have any updates on timeline?

posts.author: jpnonnet (jeanpierre.nonnet@photon.com)
posts.cookedHtml:
100% agree and have very similar use case to the one listed out by Caroline McKinnon back in Aug 2021. My main table view is more like an admin view and would be so valuable to me if I could as the admin hide this view from the every day users.

@Julietteb //Monday team you mentioned the ETA for this would be 2024 do we have an update, is this feature on schedule?

Best, JP

posts.author: MMatharu (Megan Matharu)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Juliette,

It seems the request regarding the ‘Main Table’ isn’t confirmed in your response. Can you please confirm this view is included on the roadmap?
For us, the main table is an admin view, there’s a ton of columns which shouldn’t be visible to every day users. Having the option to make the ‘Main Table’ hidden, along with other views, would be advantageous.

posts.author: Usernamegame (Jake N)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes. As an admin, I need to be able to hide the main view or any views

posts.author: bellevue (A Carl)
posts.cookedHtml: This is so critical for it to be clean, intuitive and manageable for different roles and teams. It’s not set up to be a scalable solution without this feature (IMO). I want to know where on the roadmap this is sitting since without this I may need to opt for a different solution. 2024 is almost here, but that’s a long 12 month window.

posts.author: Cyrille (L)
posts.cookedHtml: There’s another issue with the board views. At the moment, every time you will add columns in your board. These columns are added automatically to all filtered views in that board. So lets say you have a view that displays only 3 columns. When the admin adds 3 more columns to the board in any view whatsoever. These 3 new columns are added to all the other views by default, unless someone removes them manually inside all those other views. ‘Not scalable’ is the keyword. Lets hope this gets fixed too or is configurable via settings.

posts.author: Jill.S (Jill S)
posts.cookedHtml:
Agree with @MMatharu - if the Hide Main Table is considered a separate feature - can you please add the link to that feature here? It is a crucial part of this feature request - if the private views being a 2024 is a separate feature request can you include a link to that as well?

Both of these are HUGE features to help adoption of this product. Currently neither private boards nor permissions meet the needs to be able to control visibility on sensitive items but not all items without having to have separate boards which hurts productivity.

Alternatively, a people column to say “lock visibility to only people in this column” without that adding to their My work/ownership would be another work around.

posts.author: Maayango (Maayan Goldstein)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi ,
I have a general Q hope you can help ,
Where can i fined the description of the solution of featured that was released?

Thanks
Maayan

posts.author: awright (Amanda)
posts.cookedHtml: Unfortunately Lock View does not prevent new columns from being added to every view. The lock view only prevents users from adding existing columns, or deleting them. It is still very time consuming to go through every view and hide new columns that are created on the main table.

posts.author: Cyrille (L)
posts.cookedHtml: @Amanda, exactly! Every time I setup a view for someone in another department I have to explain why their view keeps changing and new columns are being added all the time. I understand Monday is rolling out Monday DB. But fixing small details like this would go a loong way for the end users.

posts.author: Posty89 (Pat rafferty)
posts.cookedHtml:
I need to be able to hide views for certain members of my team. Without this it is not a clean solution.

It seems a lot of people asking for this and is a very important feature for people.

I am trying to sell this solution to my work and without this feature it is very hard

Please can you give us a better date than sometime in 2024

posts.author: Cyrille (L)
posts.cookedHtml: @Amanda We received the feature on our account were new columns are not added by default on all views. This is really great! Next up, the ability to have personal views. And to be able to reorder them ‘à la’ ///“insert your favorite web browser here///”.

posts.author: alexross (Alex Ross)
posts.cookedHtml:
With the new hiding feature for subitem columns this request is crucial.

We’re now able to create pretty sophisticated boards/views, keeping lot’s of info on one board.

Locking views & their order for all the board users + hiding main table for all, except owners of the board, would be great.

posts.author: MichaelC (Michael Cannon)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Juliette!

Now that we’re in 2024, is there a better idea as to when the private views feature will be rolling out? Really need to be able ro limit who can see the ‘Main Table’.

posts.author: DeWua (Dorota)
posts.cookedHtml:
I fully agree that the views in Monday should have more features:

  • option to hide the Main Table (at the very least)
  • private views
  • option to add views wherever you want, not only at the end
  • option to move views to the left/right
  • option to highlight views with colours

Is there a chance to see any of these features implemented any time soon?

posts.author: Mona_rms (Mona Chaoui)
posts.cookedHtml: Nothing happened yet, no?

posts.author: EdGra (Ed)
posts.cookedHtml: A vital and pretty fundamental requirement this. Everyone should have control over what their different team members see versus what an admin sees. this is so fundamental to any online, team / group based service / platform. Please get this implemented urgently.

posts.author: Marlene (Marlene)
posts.cookedHtml:
was this relesead or not?
i dont find it in my account…

posts.author: NorthStarConsultants (Oliver Flood)
posts.cookedHtml:
SO TRUE.
Even to take it to another new view of tabs - how great would it be if we could view them in a row that would be like a menu - much easier for others to see what is there.
The restrictions to user when NOT using larger screens is always going to be a problem.

posts.author: Fentor (Julian L)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Juliette,

any news on that private views feature?

Thanks!

posts.author: YSteinberg (Yoel S)
posts.cookedHtml:
+1,000,000
this would be a game changer
we are halfway thru 2024 @Julietteb

posts.author: Horizonsong (Karo Zieminski)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Monday Team
I’m administering multiple boards for a large team. As you will see on the screenshot, We’ve created a template for the Item Card view. I would like to lock it, so that only I and selected peers can resize or move the widgets.

Screenshot 2023-08-02 at 15.10.311070×1037 54.8 KB

posts.author: NRSSH (Nav)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi Zaro, Thanks for bringing this up - we’ve found in the past people can accidentally resize the window to super tiny or they delete it - and we have to spend time remaking the item card again. On large boards, item cards are needed to access columns quickly so we have to keep remaking these item cards. Thanks

posts.author: MHaigh (Matthew Haigh)
posts.cookedHtml:
We use views quite extensively, and they are great… apart from when you have numerous users working on the board and they dont understand that if they filter and press SAVE TO VIEW, it impacts everyone…

… Monday dev team… PLEASE can to EACH VIEW and OPTIONS PAGE just like we have with each board. If you can then add an option for “Only BOARD OWNERS can SAVE TO VIEW” or “Members of THIS TEAM can SAVE TO VIEW”…

This would mean the default FILTER/PERSON/SORT/GROUPS can be set by Owners, end users can still use the filters etc, but the SAVE TO VIEW and CLEAR FILTER buttons should be greyed out or not available to stop them accidentally changing the view.

PLEASE this would make views much more manageable in enterprise environments,

posts.author: jmcquillin (James McQuillin)
posts.cookedHtml:
Private views / Hidden Views
Where can we see and monitor the status of this feature request?

posts.author: Julietteb (Juliette )
posts.cookedHtml: This feature is released! You can find more about our released features in our whats new page.
